title: AI First Software Engineer
company_focus:
Digital marketplace for used vehicles and wholesale
remarketing
description:
Who We Are:

At OPENLANE we make wholesale easy so our customers can be
more successful.

We’re a technology company building the world’s most
advanced—and uncomplicated—digital marketplace for used
vehicles.

We’re a data company helping customers buy and sell smarter
with clear, actionable insights they can understand and use.

And we’re an innovation company accelerating the future of
wholesale remarketing through curiosity, collaboration, and
an entrepreneurial spirit.

Our Values:

Driven Waybuilders. We pursue challenges that inspire us to
build, create, and innovate.

Relentless Curiosity. We seek to understand and improve our
customers’ experience.

Smart Risk-Taking. We transform risk into progress through
data, experience, and intuition.

Fearless Ownership. We deliver what we promise and learn
along the way.


We’re Looking For:

We’re hiring AI-first Software Engineers to build, evolve,
and scale OPENLANE’s U.S. marketplace platforms.

These roles are for engineers who already treat AI as a
first-class development tool, not a novelty. You’ll use AI
to move faster, ship higher-quality software, and reduce
operational and development toil, while still applying
strong engineering judgment, ownership, and systems
thinking.

You’ll work on customer-facing marketplace experiences and
core platform services, partnering closely with Product,
Data, Infrastructure, and SRE teams to deliver resilient,
scalable systems.

AI is an accelerator. Engineering ownership still matters.

Why OPENLANE:

- Work on real-time, high-value marketplace systems at scale
- Be part of a major platform modernization and AI
  transformation
- Use AI as a first-class development tool—not an experiment
- Collaborate with experienced engineers, product leaders, and
  platform teams
- Grow in an environment that values learning, ownership, and
  impact

You Are:

- AI-forward. You already use AI tools to enhance development
  speed, quality, and efficiency, and you continuously adapt
  as tooling evolves.
- Product-minded. You think beyond code and understand how
  technical decisions impact customers and business
  outcomes.
- Ownership-driven. You take responsibility for systems end-
  to-end, including reliability and operational health.
- Technically curious. You enjoy learning new technologies and
  thoughtfully evaluating emerging approaches.
- Quality-focused. You value clean, maintainable code, strong
  testing practices, and production-ready engineering
  standards.

You Bring:

Core Engineering Skills

- Experience building and supporting production software
  systems
- Strong understanding of APIs, distributed systems, and
  cloud-native architecture
- Comfort working in modern CI/CD environments
- Experience with observability, monitoring, and incident
  response
- Ability to own features and systems end-to-end

AI-First Mindset

- Actively uses AI development tools (code copilots, AI
  debuggers, test generators)
- Curious about applying AI to real customer and business
  problems
- Able to critically evaluate AI outputs instead of blindly
  accepting them
- Comfortable learning and adapting in a fast-changing tool
  landscape

Technical Experience (One or More)

- Backend development (Java, Kotlin, Go, Python, .NET, Ruby,
  or similar)
- Frontend experience (React, TypeScript) is a plus
- AWS or similar cloud platforms
- Microservices and event-driven architectures
- SQL and data-driven applications

You Will:

- Design, build, and operate scalable services that power U.S.
  marketplace workflows such as Buy Now, 45M, Simulcast, and
  DealerBlock
- Develop APIs and backend services on the modern, cloud-
  native, AI-enabled platform
- Embed AI-driven capabilities into marketplace features
  including pricing logic, negotiation flows, and workflow
  automation
- Use AI tools to accelerate coding, testing, debugging, and
  documentation while applying strong engineering judgment
- Partner with Product to translate business needs into
  durable, scalable technical solutions
- Participate in on-call rotations and support live production
  systems
- Improve system reliability, performance, security, and
  observability
- Contribute to technical direction, engineering standards,
  and long-term platform evolution

How You’ll Use AI in These Roles:

- AI-assisted coding and refactoring
- AI-generated test coverage and validation
- AI-supported debugging and root-cause analysis
- AI-assisted documentation and knowledge sharing
- Applying AI tools to reduce repetitive work and increase
  engineering leverage

Who You Will Work With:

Reporting to an Engineering Manager, this role collaborates
closely with Product Managers, Data Engineers,
Infrastructure, and Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) teams
on a daily basis


Must Have’s:

- 3+ years of experience building and supporting production
  software systems
- Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a
  related technical field (or equivalent practical
  experience)
- Experience building and supporting production software
  systems
- Backend development (Java, Kotlin, Go, Python, .NET, Ruby,
  or similar)
- Strong understanding of APIs, distributed systems, and
  cloud-native architecture
- Comfort working in modern CI/CD environments
- Experience with observability, monitoring, and incident
  response
- Ability to own features and systems end-to-end
- Actively uses AI development tools (code copilots, AI
  debuggers, test generators)
- Curious about applying AI to real customer and business
  problems
- Able to critically evaluate AI outputs instead of blindly
  accepting them
- Comfortable learning and adapting in a fast-changing tool
  landscape

Nice to Have’s:

- Frontend experience (React, TypeScript)
- AWS or similar cloud platforms
- Microservices and event-driven architectures
- SQL and data-driven applications
- Marketplace, e-commerce, or high-volume transaction systems
- Reliability engineering or SRE experience
- Experience modernizing or migrating legacy systems

What We Offer:

- Competitive pay
- Medical, dental, and vision benefits with employer HSA
  contributions (US) and FSA options (US)
- Immediately vested 401K (US) or RRSP (Canada) with company
  match
- Paid Vacation, Personal, and Sick Time
- Paid maternity and paternity leave (US)
- Employer-paid short-term disability, long-term disability,
  life insurance, and AD&D (US)
- Robust Employee Assistance Program
- Employer paid Leap into Service Day to volunteer
- Tuition Reimbursement for eligible programs
- Opportunities to expand your skill set and share your
  knowledge across a publicly traded, global organization
- Company culture of internal promotions, diverse career
  paths, and meaningful advancement

Sound like a match? Apply Now - We can't wait to hear from
you!


Compensation Range of

Annual Salary: $110,000.00 - $130,000.00 USD

(Depending on experience, skill set, qualifications, and
other relevant factors.)
responsibilities: Design, build, and operate scalable services that power U.S. marketplace workflows
Develop APIs and backend services on the modern, cloud-native, AI-enabled platform
Embed AI-driven capabilities into marketplace features including pricing logic, negotiation flows, and workflow automation
Use AI tools to accelerate coding, testing, debugging, and documentation while applying strong engineering judgment
Partner with Product to translate business needs into durable, scalable technical solutions
Participate in on-call rotations and support live production systems
Improve system reliability, performance, security, and observability
Contribute to technical direction, engineering standards, and long-term platform evolution
ai_classification_reasoning:
This role is primarily a software engineering position that
uses AI tools to accelerate development work (AI-assisted
coding, testing, debugging). The role involves building
marketplace systems and APIs, not building, training, fine-
tuning, or deploying AI/ML models. AI is used as a
productivity tool for software engineering, not as the core
deliverable.
